title: Lace extension file (.laceext)
description: TOML structure for Lace extensions. Conforms to lace-extensions.md. The TOML is parsed into JSON; this schema validates the resulting JSON shape. Rule body and function body content is opaque at this level — the extension processor parses those strings against the rule body grammar in lace-extensions.md §5.

name.description: Extension namespace prefix used in runVars and emit targets (extensions §9). camelCase identifier (no hyphens, underscores, or other punctuation) so qualified function calls `extName.fnName(...)` parse as a single IDENT on each side of the dot.

version.description: Extension version (semver recommended but not enforced).

require.description: Names of other extensions this one depends on (extensions §2.2). Presence is enforced at startup; load order doesn't matter. The depending extension gains read access to the required extensions' runVars via the `require["name"]` base in rule bodies (§9.1), and its rules default-order after required extensions at shared hooks (§8.1.1).

FieldDefMap.description: Map of field name → field definition. Field name must be IDENT-shaped (parser enforces non-keyword via grammar, see notes/grammar.md).

type.description: Built-in type name or custom type name from [types]. Built-ins: string, int, float, bool, null, any, string?, array<T>, map<K, V>.

default.description: Default value as a string (TOML stores defaults as strings; coerced to declared type at use).

required.description: If true, validator emits error when field is absent and extension is active.

ResultActionsMap.description: Map of action key → declared type. The key (e.g. 'notifications') is appended to result.actions.

ResultTypesMap.description: Custom result type declarations referenced by [result.actions.*].type.

TypesSection.description: Custom type definitions usable in schema and result type references (extensions §3.2).

oneOf.description: Tagged union with a `tag` discriminator field per variant.

params.description: Parameter names (without leading $; the interpreter prefixes).

body.description: Function body in the rule body language (extensions §5). Opaque at this schema level; parsed by the extension processor.

name.description: Rule name (used for diagnostics; not addressable by other rules).

items.pattern: ^(before script|script|before call|call|before expect|expect|before check|check|before assert|assert|before store|store)( (after|before) [a-z][A-Za-z0-9]*)*$

on.description: Hook points the rule registers for (extensions §8). Each entry may carry ordering qualifiers of the form `(after|before) <extName>`. Example: 'check after laceNotifications before laceMetrics'. Grammar: <hook> (' ' ('after' | 'before') ' ' <ext-name>)*. Extension names are camelCase IDENTs. See §8.1.1.

body.description: Rule body in the rule body language (extensions §5). Opaque at this schema level.
